concerns.text: T070 incorrectly describes evaluating "inconsistency‑detection component" on real-world validation set when FR-031b and SC-031b explicitly require evaluating "extraction accuracy component" — this is a fundamental scope mismatch that would validate the wrong metric.
Task IDs T001-T100 in tasks.md overlap with Task IDs in plan.md, creating traceability issues and potential execution conflicts.  The numbering scheme needs to be reconciled.
Tasks T096/T097 create checksums in `output/` and `manifest.json` but do not update the `state/*.yaml` file as required by Constitution Principle V, violating versioning discipline.

T076/T096 and T077/T097 create duplicate checksum/manifest tasks recording to different locations (`output/checksums.txt` vs `data/checksums.txt`) without clarifying which is authoritative — this creates ambiguity for Constitution Principle III compliance.
SC-001 and SC-031b have conflicting success metrics for extraction accuracy on the real-world validation set (95% vs. 85%/75%). This undermines testability.
Plan.md T005 requires verifying Constitution Principles I-VII in CI, but no corresponding task exists in tasks.md to perform this verification.
T031 depends on T062, creating a circular dependency between implementation and execution tasks. This could lead to build or test failures.
The plan adds a Bonferroni correction requirement for FR-005b sensitivity analysis that is not mentioned in the spec or success criteria, representing an unapproved scope expansion.
T099 implements governance invalidation but does not include a task to update the `state/*.yaml` timestamp as required by Constitution Principle V.
The Quickstart Guide uses Docker, which may not satisfy the reproducibility requirement of Constitution Principle I without additional verification of the venv setup.
